entry.title: Reconciliation issue in ABST2
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert,

I am facing some recon issue in ABST2 between GL balance and Asset Balance.

As per the above screenshot, 

5000129 & 5000139- Accumulated Depreciation GL

5000140- Asset Main GL

Kindly guide me how to compare the above GL account with FS10N & Asset and also how to fix this recon issue.

Appreciate your assistance on this.

 

Thanks,

Shreekumar

entry.title: Manage WorkForce API Access in S/4 HANA Public Edition

Greetings,

I'm currently developing a mobile application using APPGyver and need to integrate specific Workforce/Employee data.

While I've encountered a forum post - [Click Here] - that mentions a dedicated CDS View for employee information, it lacks certain data fields I require. Specifically, I'm searching for:

ΓÇó Field 1: Manager of the Employee

ΓÇó Field 2: Cost Center of the Employee

Any guidance or direction to access these fields would be greatly appreciated.

 

Regards

Rahul Jain

We tried a lot to find out the root cause for the same, but couldn't get the reason.

Mrunalini

entry.title: Re: SAC Validation Rules should be checked while running...

yes this is exactly what I was thinking. Thank you!

My users work on Private versions, but I will use a support technical public version to properly prepopulate the scenario leveraging the fact that Validation rules are checked while publishing. 

Thank you for your support!

entry.title: How to find the ID for FindById (SAP GUI Scripting)
entry.content:
All SAP GUI Script developers have to use a tool to determine the IDs of the screen elements in order to make the script interact with these fields. There are the SAP GUI Script Recorder (part of SAP GUI), ScreenReader.exe provided by SAP (note 1441550 - SAP GUI scripting: Sample applications - SAP for Me) and Scripting Tracker by Stefan Schnell. The feature is also supported by SAP GUI Scripting, but you need to create a little script. Here is a simple script example and a demonstration video.

Short presentation of what does the script:

Create this file on your laptop (ScreenReader.vbs):

 

dim stopScript
dim SapGuiApp
dim sess

Set Wrp = WScript.CreateObject("SapROTWr.SapROTWrapper")
Set SapGui = Wrp.GetROTEntry ("SAPGUI")
If SapGui Is Nothing Then
  MsgBox "SAP Logon/SAP GUI for Windows must be started first (manually)"
  WScript.quit 0
End If
Set SapGuiApp = sapgui.GetScriptingEngine ' GuiApplication

set conn = SapGuiApp.Children(0)
set sess = conn.Children(0)
' or use InputBox to select one session? (see https://stackoverflow.com/questions/71624238/find-sap-gui-session-opened-for-given-sap-system)
set window = sess.ActiveWindow

' Connect the events to procedures
Call WScript.ConnectObject(sess,"sess_")
window.elementVisualizationMode = true ' this enables the connection of the Hit event to the connected method

' Endless loop until window is closed or user click a field > InputBox requests the end
stopScript = false
j = 1
Do While stopScript = false
  j = j + 1
  WScript.Sleep 200
Loop
Set sess = Nothing

Sub sess_Hit(Session, Component, InnerObject)
  input = inputbox("Enter to continue the script, Cancel/Escape to leave.", , component.Id)
  If input = "" Then
    stopScript = True
  End If
End Sub

 

Log manually into SAP GUI (user and password).

Run the VBS file (double-click it from Windows File Explorer).

Click on any field of the SAP GUI screen and a screen displays the field ID that you may copy.

Press Enter or click OK in this screen to continue with other fields, or press Escape or click Cancel to leave the script.

Currently, the script works only with the first session opened, but you may adapt it to find a session (see here how to loop at the opened sessions https://stackoverflow.com/questions/71624238/find-sap-gui-session-opened-for-given-sap-system)

Also, the script can work only if the SAP system is configured to allow SAP GUI Scripting and if you selected the Scripting component when you installed SAP GUI for Windows (Mac also).

References: some time ago, I had answered a question about the same topic, how to find the ID for FindById.

entry.title: Re: The Central Communication Center in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Hi,

please stop filling the Communication Center with messages every half hour telling me, that "Credentials will be deleted automatically soon" (Categorie "Customer Remote Logon Depot").
Also please provide possibility to select and delete multiple messages.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Call API on Presentation Server using CL_HTTP_CLIENT
entry.content: Can destination SAPHTTP be accessed via CL_HTTP_CLIENT? I always thought, CL_HTTP_CLIENT communicates through HTTP-destinations of type G and H, but SAPHTTP is an RFC-destination (type T), which needs to be accessed via CALL FUNCTION. (The RFC call transfers the URL and other parameters to the saphttp.exe executable, which then makes the HTTP request.)

Hossein

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM webhook and SOAP protocol
entry.content:
Hello

We are configuring the integration between SAP Cloud ALM and ServiceNow for alerts. Could you please specify if SAP Cloud ALM supports the external API within SOAP or only the REST?

BR


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM webhook and SOAP protocol
entry.content:
The full explanation is available in KBA 3484463 - SAP Cloud ALM External API Management and SOAP for Ticketing systems

In summary, the official guides for configuring integration between SAP Cloud ALM External API Management and external ticketing software outline that REST is a requirement. There is no SOAP support at this time.

entry.title: Revolutionizing Business with SAP AI Technologies: A Comprehensive Overview
entry.content:
In the modern business landscape, artificial intelligence (AI) has become a crucial driver of innovation and efficiency. SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, integrates AI into its platforms and applications to transform business processes, enhance decision-making, and provide advanced analytics. This blog explores how various SAP technologies leverage AI to offer comprehensive solutions for diverse business needs. Here are some key aspects of SAP technology in AI:

SAP Leonardo: Driving Digital Innovation
SAP leverages artificial intelligence (AI) across its platforms and applications to enhance business processes, automate tasks, and provide advanced analytics. Here are some key aspects of SAP technology in AI: A leading automotive manufacturer implemented SAP Leonardo to streamline its production processes. By integrating IoT sensors with AI algorithms, the company achieved real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance of its machinery.

SAP Conversational AI: Enhancing Customer Service
SAP Conversational AI is a platform for building chatbots and virtual assistants. It enables businesses to create AI-driven conversational interfaces that can automate customer service, sales, and other interactions. A global retail chain adopted SAP Conversational AI to improve its customer service. The AI-driven chatbots handled routine inquiries, provided product recommendations, and facilitated online purchases.

SAP Data Intelligence: Optimizing Data Management
SAP Data Intelligence is an end-to-end data management solution that integrates AI and machine learning capabilities. It helps businesses harness the power of their data by connecting, discovering, and orchestrating data across different sources. A financial services firm used SAP Data Intelligence to consolidate and analyze large volumes of data from disparate sources. Machine learning models were employed to detect fraud and predict market trends.

SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation (RPA): Automating Routine Tasks
SAP Intelligent RPA combines AI and machine learning to automate repetitive tasks and processes. It allows businesses to increase efficiency and reduce errors by automating routine tasks. A healthcare provider implemented SAP Intelligent RPA to automate administrative tasks such as patient data entry and billing processes.

SAP S/4HANA: Transforming ERP with AI
SAP S/4HANA incorporates AI and machine learning to enhance core ERP functionalities. It offers intelligent automation, predictive analytics, and advanced data processing capabilities to improve decision-making and operational efficiency. A multinational corporation migrated to SAP S/4HANA to leverage AI-driven insights for financial planning and supply chain management.

SAP Analytics Cloud: Advancing Business Intelligence
SAP Analytics Cloud integrates AI and machine learning to provide advanced analytics, forecasting, and business intelligence. It offers smart insights and automated analytics to help businesses make informed decisions. A retail company used SAP Analytics Cloud to analyze customer data and predict purchasing behaviors, enabling targeted marketing campaigns.

SAP Ariba: Enhancing Procurement with AI
SAP Ariba leverages AI to improve procurement and supply chain management. It uses machine learning algorithms to predict supply chain disruptions, optimize procurement processes, and enhance supplier management. A manufacturing firm adopted SAP Ariba to optimize its procurement processes, utilizing AI to predict supply chain disruptions and manage suppliers effectively.

SAP SuccessFactors: Streamlining HR Management
SAP SuccessFactors incorporates AI to enhance human resources management. It uses AI to automate talent acquisition, employee onboarding, and performance management processes. A technology company used SAP SuccessFactors to automate talent acquisition and performance management processes.

SAP C/4HANA: Revolutionizing Customer Experience
SAP C/4HANA integrates AI to improve customer experience management. It provides AI-driven insights and automation for marketing, sales, commerce, and customer service functions. An e-commerce company adopted SAP C/4HANA to provide personalized customer experiences through AI-driven insights.

Use Cases of SAP AI Technology:
- Predictive Maintenance: Using AI to predict equipment failures and schedule maintenance proactively.
- Demand Forecasting: Leveraging machine learning to predict customer demand and optimize inventory levels.
- Customer Service Automation: Implementing chatbots to handle routine customer inquiries and improve response times.
- Fraud Detection: Using AI algorithms to detect and prevent fraudulent activities in financial transactions.

Conclusion - SAPΓÇÕs integration of AI into its products and solutions enables businesses to harness the power of artificial intelligence to drive innovation, efficiency, and growth.


Sources:
SAP Leonardo Overview: https://www.sap.com/products/leonardo.html
SAP Conversational AI: https://www.sap.com/products/conversational-ai.html
SAP Data Intelligence: https://www.sap.com/products/data-intelligence.html
SAP Intelligent RPA: https://www.sap.com/products/intelligent-rpa.html
SAP S/4HANA: https://www.sap.com/products/s4hana.html
SAP Analytics Cloud: https://www.sap.com/products/analytics-cloud.html
SAP Ariba: https://www.sap.com/products/ariba.html
SAP SuccessFactors: https://www.sap.com/products/successfactors.html
SAP C/4HANA: https://www.sap.com/products/c4hana.html
These sources provide detailed information about SAP's AI technologies and their applications in various business contexts.

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content:
Hello, my name is Fabrice L├│pez.

I have been working as an SAP consultant since 2008, right after they acquired Business Objects.

Although I continue to work with all the tools in the Business Objects platform, over the past five years, I have gained extensive knowledge in cloud solutions, particularly SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Datasphere.

I'm happy to be part of this community and look forward to connecting with you all.

Regards;

Fabrice

entry.title: Re: How to identify a manager based on position hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi @karenperez  ,

May you please suggest a way to exclude contingent child positions from the Get Number of Child Positions? 

We have a requirement wherein a user should only be identified as a manager if his/her direct reports are all regular employees or combination of regular employees and contingent workers. If the the user has only contingent workers as his direct reports, then he/she should not be identified as a manager.

We tried adding addition parameters to the business rule you proposed to only include regular child positions but it does not work. It still identifies users with only contingent workers direct reports as manager in the custom field.

Thank you!

Regards,

Jan Erik

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Data Import on Locked Versions
entry.content:
Hi friends,

I need to enable the Planning Standard User to execute data loads into a locked version, such as "Actuals".

Currently, the Planning Standard User Role has full privileges except for Planning Professional privileges. However, when this user attempts to execute an import job, they receive a "Data locking violation" notification.

One solution is to set the version to restricted, but that isn't practical for "Actuals," as it must remain locked at all times.

What other options can we explore to achieve this goal?

Kind regards,

Ismail

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM RFUI Design Mode in Personas is difficult to use
entry.content:
Hi,

the RFUI team has been notified and will discuss the possibility.

Best Regards!

entry.title: PR generated but Vendor not picked
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have one material code, after running the MRP PR has been generated but the PR generated still 31.12.2024 has picked the vendor code and PR generated for forecast after 01.01.2025 has not picked the vendor. See in attached snap, PR generated on date 30.12.2024 has picked the vendor whereas PR generated on 13.01.2025 don't. Can you pls tell me what I have missed and that needs to correct so that these future PR can also pick the vendor code.

Snap attached for ref.

entry.title: Re: How to allocate revenue in Plan
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

In Profit center allocation context, allocation is only possible via GL account of types X (Balance sheet accounts) and N (Non-operating expenses/income). There is chapter in documentation related:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f0d042517a434458b171ff5867b18445/1e3d7bed916b414a969aa690b89afe0c.html?locale=en-US&state=TEST&version=2408.500

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: ClientId of Document Extraction inside SAP Process Au...
entry.content:
I think it's not DIE(it sounds strange once you call die.). It's DOX.
Can you check the list from GET_CLIENTS? Then try to call those APIs one after another.

entry.title: Re: Call API on Presentation Server using CL_HTTP_CLIENT
entry.content:
> (or another method if it exists)?

Another option might be the following: as you are accessing a hardware connected to an USB slot, you have probably written a C/C++ component that accesses the USB device via the driver provided by the hardware vendor?

In that case, why not call the C/C++ component directly from ABAP (without detour via HTTP Client & HTTP Server on the frontend)? This is actually not so difficult. Basic outline:

  1. Create an SM59 destination of type T with "start on frontend", similar to how the SAPHTTP destination looks like, but instead of "saphttp.exe" you enter your own executable name.
  2. Put the functionality, which access the USB device driver, into an executable and link it with the NW RFC Library (sapnwrfc.dll).
  3. In order to be invocable from ABAP, that executable needs to fulfill two prerequisites:
    - From main(), it needs to call the function RfcStartServer(), passing in the command line parameters it receives when being started. (SAPGUI will start the executable as a child process, passing in the details necessary for connecting to the current ABAP user session.)
    - It needs to define and implement the interface of one (or more) remote-enabled function module.
    The details for this are described in the NW RFC SDK Programming Guide, chapter 5 (in particular section 5.3), which can be downloaded from https://support.sap.com/nwrfcsdk
    The interface would define the inputs that the USB device needs and the outputs returned by the USB device. Can be simple strings, ints or floats, or even tables or binary blobs.

Then you would need to install that executable on the end-user frontends, for example together with the device driver DLL (which is needed in any case), and then your ABAP program on the Application Server can access the end-user's USB device with a simple

 

DATA: error_detail(120).

CALL FUNCTION 'MY_FUNCTION' DESTINATION 'DEST_FROM_STEP1'
  EXPORTING
    "...inputs needed by USB device
  IMPORTING
    "...results from USB device
  TABLES
    "...if any
  EXCEPTIONS
    SYSTEM_FAILURE = 1 MESSAGE error_detail
    COMMUNICATION_FAILURE = 2 MESSAGE error_detail.

IF sy-subrc EQ 1.
  "e.g. USB device not plugged in etc.
ELSEIF sy-subrc EQ 2.
  "network connection between app server and frontend broke down
ENDIF.

entry.title: Auto XLD , user mapping between different user Ids
entry.content:
While using the Cross-landscape distribution UI function via charm in the Solman SC4 system for China, as a prerequisite, we need to create a target document for SS6(CHINA) to distribute the transport from SS4 (S/4 HANA SYSTEM) to SS6 (CHINA SYSTEM). At the same time, when we carry out the automated cross-landscape distribution,
as per the rule, Target TR will create in the same change document and add it to the transport management block.
Here system is unable to generate the target TR in the change document with the user ID U0*******because it doesn't exist in the China system,where the ID is CN******.So thus , we are keen to know in how the automated XLD UI selects the User ID in the backend to produce the target TR. #XLD #AutoXLD

entry.title: Re: Call API on Presentation Server using CL_HTTP_CLIENT
entry.content: What happens if you run that call several times in parallel?

entry.title: SAP Business One .The connection to SQL Server failed. Check the log for details.
entry.content:
I first setup SAP Bussiness One(B11000_2405-70004131). The connection to SQL Server failed. Check the log for details. Note: I am not using "sa" user, I use another user that was created on SQL server, whose role is the same as "sa" user.

I have verified: 
Port and firewall , ODBC is OK, TCP/IP is enabled under SQL Native Client Configuration, the SQL Server Browser service is running on the SQL Server.Please help to  fixed it .

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud TDD Scope Item Activation 6W3
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.
Yes, there are dependencies between scope items in Transportation Management.

Please refer to the following SAP Help Portal document:https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1ae64e2df9d94766b6315a06208c7487/d13f095cd082429980a7123b6ad0ac5b.html?state=DRAFT&q=scope%20item%206w3:

6W1 - External Transportation Planning

6W3 - Transportation Execution

6W4 - Freight Settlement

The listed scope items must be activated together with 6W1.
6W2 - Manual Transportation Planning

6W3 - Transportation Execution

6W4 - Freight Settlement

The listed scope items must be activated together with 6W2.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Unavailability of cXML Type System under TPM configuration in SAP integration suite
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have few B2B scenarios which using cXML / Commerce XML structures available in integration advisor.
But, In B2B/Trading Partner Management on SAP Integration Suite, I can't able to find any type system in the dropdown list like as cXML for creating identifier.


Can anyone please help me on this for getting cXML as type system in that dropdown list, So that I can configure those scenarios by using TPM.

Please refer attached screenshot as reference.

Regards,
Amar

entry.title: Re: Longest Query Response Time Since Startup (msec) - Al...
entry.content:
Hi,

CMS queries are used to get details about objects in BOE landscape (Users, Folders, Documents, Universes ...)
They are used for instance to get content when navigating in BILaunchpad and CMC, but it's separate from database query executed by products like Analysis Office or WebIntelligence.
These queries are supposed to be quite fast since it is only fetching metadata, and they mostly retrieve small amount of data.

You can add some parameters in CMS command line to get traces of long running queries.
This is explained in SAP Note https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2743951
You could start by configuring a minimum duration of 10 seconds, to limit amount of these CMS query logs, and see how often it happens.

You may also want to look at increasing size of objects cache in CMS.(see KBA https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/1981894)


Regards,
Loic

entry.title: Re: Extract whole text from Document Information extraction
entry.content:
Thanks Tobias.

I have raised the request, I think it will be an improvement, specially for generative AI processing.

 https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/325306

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Great to have someone experienced like you here @FabriceLopez and hope you are enjoying SAP Community!

entry.title: Unable to update impact, urgency and priority in SNOW via CALM Mapping
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We cannot update the impact, urgency, and priority fields in SNOW using the standard Service-Now API in SAP cloud ALM. However, when a ticket is created in Service from a Health Monitoring alert, we can populate most fields in SNOW using rule-based mapping. Could you please assist us with how to populate the impact, urgency, and subsequently priority fields in SNOW? Please refer to the attachments for more details.

Thanks and Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to update impact, urgency and priority in SNOW...
entry.content:
The full answer is available in KBA 3484531 - How to configure SAP Cloud ALM to use specific values for ticket fields when ticket is being created?

In summary, you need to update your mapping so that it includes the fields you want to edit as "destinations", and there must be some kind of value inserted in the mapping. That value can be a placeholder, or use one of the "choices" logic blocks to put in specific values depending on other factors (e.g. the state of the related alert, etc.).

In your specific scenario, the ServiceNow Table API documentation mentions that the "impact", "urgency", and "priority" attributes are available and accept numbers (1, 2, 3, etc.) to reflect their statuses. You will need to update your mapping so that those attributes are added as "destinations", and then probably use the "choices" blocks to give them appropriate values based on the mappings. You can examine one of the "built-in" ServiceNow mappings, it updates the "impact" value already and you can use that as a guide for updating those other field attributes.

entry.title: Re: With SAP Quartz Dark Theme when I open a Message in B...
entry.content: Thank you for the answer

entry.title: Re: Universal ID Problem with Linked Accounts
entry.content: This is not working, Please help I have my exam in 4 days

entry.title: SAP EWM can handle Batch Specific Unit of Measure!
entry.content:
While already delivered by end of 2023, this is a relevant topic for Mill Products industries, and I haven't seen this being rolled out already: One of the big highlights of SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 release is the ability to use Batch specific Unit of Measure (UoM). Our warehousing solution is used across 24 industry sectors, and in some cases (for example, pharmaceutical, chemical, steel, or paper), the composition or attributes of products vary to some degree. Now with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 release, Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA, you can use batch-specific units of measure to handle these types of products throughout the entire logistics chain.

When discussing such products, it is important to make a distinction between proportion units of measure and product units of measure.

  • Proportion units are used in chemical, food, and pharmaceutical industries to manage active ingredients in a carrier material. What is important to note is that there we have to make a distinction between the Filling Quantity and the Contents (a.k.a. the Active Ingredients). The Filling Quantity is the Base unit of measure which is the logistical relevant unit of measure. The active ingredients meanwhile are not logistically relevant units of measure. For example, in the case of a box of juice, the Filling Quantity (and the Base unit of measure) is 1L of juice. The active ingredients (which are not logistically relevant) are the proportions of ingredients within that juice, such as 35% fruit extract, 2% acid, 30% water, etc.

 

 

  •  Product units are used mainly in steel, paper, wood, and textile industries to handle the fixed relationship between the number of pieces and the base unit of measure, for example, kilogram, for each batch. When talking about product unit, the Product unit of measure is counted in Pieces (e.g. 1 Piece of metal bar, or 10 Pieces of wood). The Base unit of measure on the other hand is the measure by which that material is quantified, such as weight or length. As an example, a roll of metal cord can be counted as 1 PC as Product unit of measure, but as a Base unit of measure, it can be counted as 50 meters of metal cord. 

 

 

Batch-specific units of measure are used to maintain proportion units and product units for these types of products because it is not feasible to use a fixed conversion factor to convert quantities of these products into various units of measure. Instead, each batch must be given an individual conversion factor.

Read the full article for all details: SAP┬« Extended Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA ... - SAP Community

entry.title: SolmanΓÁÈADSπü╕πü«RFCσ«¢σàêµÜùσÂ╖σÔÚπü½πüñπüãπüª
entry.content:
πüèΣ╕ÚΦÒ▒πü½πü¬πüúπüªπüèπéèπü╛πüÕπÇé

µÃ▓ΘíÔπü½πüñπüãπüªS/4HANA DIGITSπéÈπü»πüÌπéüΦñçµò░πü«SAPπé╜πâ¬πâÑπâ╝πé╖πâºπâ│πéÈσ░ÃσàÑΣ║êσ«ÜπüºπüÕπÇé

πé╗πé¡πâÑπâ¬πâÁπéúΦªüΣ╗╢πüÍπéÊσà¿πé╡πâ╝πâÉΘÚôΘÇÜΣ┐íπéÈµÜùσÂ╖σÔÚπüÕπéÍσ┐àΦªüπüÔπüéπéèπü╛πüÕπÇé

SolmanΓçõADSπü«PERSON_NAMEΘÇÜΣ┐íτ╡ÔΦ╖»πü«SSL/TLSσÔÚπéÈµñ£Φ¿╝Σ╕¡πüºπüÕπÇé

PERSON_NAMEΦ¿¡σ«Üσ«ÔΣ║Áσ╛ÔπÇüTrcd:SM59πü½πüªσñÚΘâ¿πü╕πü«httpΘÇÜΣ┐íπé½πâÁπé┤πâ¬πüºΣ╕ÍΦ¿Ìπü«RFCσ«¢σàêπüÔτÕ╗ΘÔ▓πüòπéÔπüªπüãπéÍπüôπü¿πéÈτó║Φ¬ìπüùπü╛πüùπüÓπÇé

πâ╗E2E_SM_SLDRTC_<SID>

πüôπü«σ«¢σàêπéÈSSL/TLSσÔÚπüÕπéÍσá┤σÉêπü»πÇüSM59πüÍπéÊσ«Üτ╛ÒπéÈσñÊµ¢┤πüÕπéÔπü░πéêπéìπüùπüãπüºπüùπéçπüÁπüÍ∩╝Ó

σ░ÜπÇüTrcd:solman_setupπü«σÁàσ«╣πéÈτó║Φ¬ìπüùπüÓπü¿πüôπéìπÇü

σ╜ôΦÒ▓RFCπü»τ«íτÉÁσ»╛Φ▒íπé╖πé╣πâÁπâáΦ¿¡σ«ÜΓÁÈPERSON_NAMEπâòπâ½Φ¿¡σ«ÜΓÁÈ6.Φ¿¡σ«Üσ«ÔΣ║ÁΓÁÈWEBπé╡πâ╝πâôπé╣Φ½ÚτÉÁπâÙπâ╝πâêΣ╜£µêÉπü«σ«ÓΦíÔπü½πéêπéèτõÓµêÉπüòπéÔπüÓπéêπüÁπüºπüÕπÇé


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SolmanΓÁÈADSπü╕πü«RFCσ«¢σàêµÜùσÂ╖σÔÚπü½πüñπüãπüª
entry.content:
πüõµÍàσ╜ôΦÇàµºÌ

σ╣│τ┤áπéêπéèπüèΣ╕ÚΦÒ▒πü½πü¬πüúπüªπüèπéèπü╛πüÕπÇé
µ£¼Σ╗╢σÁàΘâ¿τó║Φ¬ìπü½πüèµÕéΘÚôπüãπüÓπüáπüìπÇüπüÒπüÁπééπüéπéèπüÔπü¿πüÁπüõπüÚπüãπü╛πüÕπÇé

πüèσòÂπüãσÉêπéÂπü¢πü½πüñπüìπü╛πüùπüªπü»πÇüΣ╕ÍΦ¿Ìσ¢₧τ¡õπüòπü¢πüªπüãπüÓπüáπüìπü╛πüÕπÇé


πü»πüãπÇüπüèΦ¬ìΦ¡Ìπü«ΘÇÜπéèπüºπÇüSM59πüÍπéÊσ«Üτ╛ÒπéÈσñÊµ¢┤πüÕπéÔπü░πüãπüãπéôπüºπüÕπÇé
SM59Γ₧₧E2E_SM_SLDRTC_<SID>Γ₧₧Logon & SecurityΓ₧₧Security Optionsπü«SSLπéÈπÇÔActiveπÇìπü½πüõΦ¿¡σ«ÜπüÂπüáπüòπüãπÇé

Σ╗ÑΣ╕èπÇéπéêπéìπüùπüÂπüèΘíÌπüãπüãπüÓπüùπü╛πüÕπÇé
SAP πé╡πâÙπâ╝πâê
πé╖πâºπéªπé╗πéñ

entry.title: Re: AUC Value is negative
entry.content:
During the settlement process there are times the AUC will go negative without the flag set then you will get error AA617.

For your case I would look at final asset and compare it to AUC, it looks like there could be a item that settled too large a number. You could try reversing and checking settlements again.

entry.title: How to connect SAP Sustainability Control Tower to SAP Analytics Cloud via API
entry.content:
There is a way to integrate SAP Analytics Cloud with SAP Sustainability Control Tower for real-time data access and dynamic visualization by establishing a live connection. This currently only supports greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions data. For more information on how to do that, you can have a look at the SAP Help for SAP Sustainability Control Tower Integrations here: SAP Analytics Cloud Integration

In addition to that, SAP Sustainability Control Tower offers APIs as another way to replicate data from your source systems into SAP Sustainability Control Tower, or to replicate data from SAP Sustainability Control Tower to your systems.

The main focus of this blog, is to help anyone out there who is looking to connect SAP Sustainability Control Tower to SAP Analytics Cloud via APIs for data replication.

Let's do this! ...in 3 parts:

  1. Setting up is needed in your Subaccount in the SAP BTP Cockpit
  2. Testing of the API can be done in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  3. Configuring the connection in SAP Analytics Cloud

 

Setting up what is needed in your Subaccount in the SAP BTP Cockpit

First, you need to go into your SAP BTP Cockpit and find the Subaccount you have set up for using SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

Check there to see if you have already enabled Cloud Foundry.

If not, you'll see a button to Enable Cloud Foundry:

When you click to Enable Cloud Foundry, a popup will come up, and you'll just have to click on Create:

After that is done, you'll need to create a Space. To do that, click on Create Space:

In the popup that will come up, give it a Space Name and click to Create:

Next, click on Entitlements on the menu in the left side of your screen.

Check in that list for the SAP Sustainability Control Tower service and verify if you already have the service sct-service-api with the standard plan listed there:

 

If it's there, you can skip this next step. 

If it's not there, proceed to add this by clicking on Edit on the top right side of your screen. That will enable a new button to Add Service Plans. Click to Add Service Plans, and you'll get the popup below:

Type in "control" in the search box, which will result in finding the service SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

Click on SAP Sustainability Control Tower, and that will display the Available Plans on the right.

Click to check the checkbox for standard - SAP Sustainability Control Tower service broker (API).

Click on the right bottom corner to Add 1 Service Plan.

When back in the Entitlements list, click on the top right of your screen to Save.

Now, you should be able to find the service in the list:

 

Next, click on Instances and Subscriptions on the left menu:

On the top right corner, you'll find the button to Create, click on that button.

In the dropdown to Select service, find SAP Sustainability Control Tower sct-service-api:

In the dropdown to select a Plan, select standard under Instances:

Give it an Instance Name, and click on Create:

You should now be seeing 1 Instance and 1 Environment together with your 1 Subscription in the Instances and Subscriptions screen:

Next, click on the Instance, to see its details:

Click on the Create button to create a Service Key.

In the popup, give it a Service Key Name and click to Create:

This is the end of the steps in the SAP BTP Cockpit, but don't close it just yet, as we'll need to come back here to check the JSON code of the Service Key for the Cliend ID and Client Secret.

 

Testing of the API in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Now, let's go to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub (https://api.sap.com/) to check if this is working!

Search for sustainability control tower:

In the search results, click on the ODATA V4 API Get Sustainability Metrics.

Once in the Get Sustainability Metrics API, click on Try Out:

 

Then, on the left menu, select a metric for which you have data in SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

In this example, I will select Environmental Metrics and then GET /GhgEmissions:

Now, click on Select Environment on the top right corner of your screen and choose to Add New Environment:

In the popup to Configure New Environment.

Give it a Display Name, and then you'll need to fill in Cliend ID and Client Secret:

This is where you need to go back to the SAP BTP Cockpit, and look at the details of the Service Key.

For that, you need to click the name of your Service Key, that is a link that will show you more details:

When you click that name of the Service Key which is a link, a popup will appear with the JSON code and in that code you'll find Client ID and Client Secret in the lines highlighted in green below:

Copy the lines in green, one at a time, to fill in the Client ID and Client Secret in the Configure New Environment popup back in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub to test the API.

Keep the SAP BTP Cockpit open, as we'll need to come back here to get a URL from this same JSON code again when configuring the connection in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Then, fill in the Landscape and Subdomain from your SCT tenant URL:

Last, click the checkbox to Apply and select the Save this environment for future sessions option.

Click to Configure:

When you click on Configure, it may take a while for the screen to react, no need to click on Configure again, just wait a little bit.

To test this, click on the Run button on the top right side of your screen:

You should get something like this:

If you get results like this to show up, this means the connection is working!

Alright, time to go to SAP Analytics Cloud...

 

Configuring the connection in SAP Analytics Cloud

In SAP Analytics Cloud, go to Connections, you can find it on the left menu, almost at the bottom:

Click on the "+" option on the top right corner:

Type "odata" in the search, to find OData Services:

Click on OData Services and the below popup will appear:

 

Start by entering a Connection Name.

To fill in the Data Service URL, you'll need to go back to the SAP BTP Cockpit one last time, and in the JSON code of the Service Key, you'll find a URL which ends with "sustainabilityMetrics":

As Authentication Type, select OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials.

By now, you already know where to find the OAuth Client ID and Secret again, in the JSON of your Service Key in the SAP BTP Cockpit.

For the Token URL, you'll have to go back to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, search for "control tower", find the ODATA V4 API called Get Sustainability Metrics, go into it and then click to Try Out. Or... you can try this direct link: https://api.sap.com/api/SustainabilityMetrics/tryout

Once you are there, you'll have to click on Select Environment, and next to the name of your Environment, there will be a pencil button you can click to check its details. In that popup you'll find the Token URL:

 

Copy the Token URL and paste it in the New OData Services Connection popup in SAP Analytics Cloud and after that you can click on the Create button.

Now your new Connection should show up in the Connections list in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Next, go to the Modeler, you can find it as a little cube icon on the left menu:

Click on Model, to create a new model, and a popup will appear:

Leave it with Start with Data selected and click Next.

Type in odata, to search for OData Services:

Click on OData Services and then you'll have to select a Connection.

In the dropdown to select a connection, you should find your new connection:

Click Next and then you'll have to set up a New Query for OData Services:

In this example, we're still considering you have data for emissions, so you can search for ghg and select GhgEmissions, for example:

After you click Next, you'll need to select data and filters.

You can drag and drop the GhgEmissions to the Selected Data:

After you click on Next, the below popup will appear with a Batch Size.

You can leave that as is and click to Create:

 

Your new model will show up, and you'll need to click on the disk in the top menu under General, to Save it.

A popup will appear where you can just click Save:

Now you can use the data in SAP Analytics Cloud!!!

Let's give it a quick try... for example, creating a story.

Go to the left menu and click the icon that looks like a chart for Stories:

Next, click on the big button to create a new Canvas.

A popup will appear where you can just click to Create:

Now you can drag and drop a Chart from the left Widgets menu into the main content section of your New Story. When you do that, a popup will appear to Select Dataset or Model:

Click on the name of your model to select it.

Next, on the right menu, you can select Measures and Dimensions:

In this example, let's select for Measures, the measure QUANTITY and for Dimensions, the dimension BUSINESSLOCATIONID.

Finally, you get a Chart in your New Story in SAP Analytics Cloud with your SAP Sustainability Control Tower data!

entry.title: Re: Universal ID Problem with Linked Accounts
entry.content: I asked sso@sap.com to relink one of my already linked P* accounts to a different email domain and it worked.

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Thanks great idea IΓÇÕll do that!

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi Vitaliy,

Getting below error.I have installed module it says requirement satisfied.

entry.title: Re: Call API on Presentation Server using CL_HTTP_CLIENT
entry.content:
That depends on your executable: every locking/synchronization that the USB device might need, needs to be implemented in the executable (e.g. using OS level semaphores etc.).

But I think it is very hard to run the call several times in parallel from within the same user session (SAPGUI session). You would need to use the ABAP parallelization mechanism via "STARTING NEW TASK" etc.

entry.title: Re: How to make a REST "PUT" call to a dynamic url and pa...
entry.content: Hi Bill,

entry.title: Re: Call API on Presentation Server using CL_HTTP_CLIENT
entry.content: I just checked with the developer: destination SAPHTTP can not be used with CL_HTTP_CLIENT. One needs to access it via CALL FUNCTION to certain function modules.

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI Scripts Recorder not available anymore - What...
entry.content:
Check the scripting Profile Parameters in TA RZ11. They should show as follows:

Profile Parameter Value
sapgui/user_scripting TRUE
sapgui/user_scripting_per_user FALSE
sapgui/user_scripting_disable_recording FALSE
sapgui/user_scripting_force_notification FALSE
sapgui/user_scripting_set_readonly FALSE
sapgui/nwbc_scripting (EHP7 onwards) FALSE

Also have a look at the SAP GUI Scripting Security Guide, chapter 4 under
https://help.sap.com/doc/97d2d0bc2ed248a4a85a0bec608704f8/760.01/en-US/sap_gui_scripting_sec_guide.pdf

entry.title: Re: Block Automatic Posting of Billing Documents to Finance in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @Babu_Lal_Limba,

Can we set other  standrd fields such as incoterms as parameter for blocking criteria?

entry.title: A Complete Guide to Comparing Versions in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Comparing different versions of data is a common scenario in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). It allows users to understand how their planned data compares to actuals, budgets, or even different forecast versions.

In SAC, versions are often treated as default filters. Users frequently select a version before performing any planning action, and most visualizations focus on insights from a single version. Consequently, comparing different versions might not be straightforward for some users. This guide will outline the current options for comparing versions and the commonly used visuals to represent these comparisons.

Please note all demos in this guide are created in the optimized story experience.

1. Using calculations in the story

If you would like to create a quick and simple version comparison, using calculation functions in the story is a good approach. This method is particularly effective with a relatively small dataset.

1.1 Using "Add Client Calculation" in the Table

This method applied to the scenario that:

  • You want to compare versions in a table
  • This comparison is only used in this table, you wonΓÇÕt use it elsewhere in this story

Follow the steps:

  1. Create a table with the measures and versions you wish to compare. In this case, I would like to compare the gross sales between actual and budget.
  2. Select the headers of two versions, right-click to open the pop-up function window, select "Add Client Calculation," and choose the calcuation method. In this case, I would like to calculate the actual/budget to get the rate of completion.
  3. A "story calculation" will appear in the "version" column. Click it to expand and check the newly created calculation, "Completion rate." Adjust the unit to a percentage if needed.
  4. Add other dimensions to the Row if needed, in this case I can also check the completion rate for different sales orgs.

Visualizing the comparison using in-cell charts and thresholds can make your table easier to understand.

 

 

1.2 Using the "Variance" Feature in Charts

This method applied to the scenario that:

  • You want to compare versions in a chart by only showing the variance from another version.
  • This comparison is only used in this chart, you wonΓÇÕt use it elsewhere in this story

Follow the steps below to achieve this:

  1. Create a chart on a specific version you would like to focus on. In this case, I create a chart showing the gross sales for different sales org for the ΓÇ£ActualΓÇÙ version.

2. In the ΓÇ£Chart Add-onsΓÇÙ section, choose ΓÇ£VarianceΓÇÙ and create a variance to compare the ΓÇ£actualΓÇÙ  V.S. ΓÇ£budgetΓÇÙ. You can choose various visualization options. In my case, I choose the Bar style to display the percentage difference.

3. There are many other common visuals to represent version differences.

However, not all charts in SAC supports variance, eg, stacked bar chart hasnΓÇÕt support variance yet. What other options we have then? LetΓÇÕs move to the next section.

 

1.3 Using Restricted Accounts in the Story Calculation

This method applied to the scenario that:

  • You want to compare versions in a chart which not supports variance, or show the comparisons directly in a table.
  • This comparison is used many times in a story, in multiple charts and tables.

Follow the steps below to achieve this:

1. Create a table to show the process and values of different calculations. In my case I would like to create a comparison between Actual and Budget on the measure gross sales.

2. Use "Add Calculation" and "Restrict Accounts" to calculate gross sales for both actual and budget. Create two restricted accounts: one for actual and one for budget.

 

 

Be sure to select the ΓÇ£Enable Constant SelectionΓÇÙ and choose version as the constant dimension.  A measure with constant selection will not be impacted by filters or drill-downs on the constant dimensions.

 

As shown in the screenshot above, the value of grossactual is the same for both the actual and budget versions. Same for the gross budget.

3. Next, create a calculated account to further calculate the above two gross sales values. In my case, I simply calculate actual/budget. You can do more calculations according to your needs.

4. Finally, you will get a value for both the actual and budget version, and the value is the same for both.

This method allows you to unlock more types of visuals to represent the differences between versions.

1.4 Dynamically Selecting Versions to Compare

This is one step forward of the solution provided in section 1.3. If the user has the requirement of enabling the viewers to select which version they would like to compare across when viewing the story, then this method is worth to try.

Similar to what we did in section 1.3, we will use restricted account and calculated account to achieve this.

However, when creating the restricted account for both actual and budget versions, instead of selecting ΓÇ£actualΓÇÙ or ΓÇ£budgetΓÇÙ for the version values, we create ΓÇ£new calculation input controlΓÇÙ for the ΓÇ£compare toΓÇÙ and ΓÇ£compare fromΓÇÙ versions.

  1. Create restricted accounts for both actual and budget versions, named gsactualdynamic and gsbudgetdynamic.

2. Create calculation input controls for both restricted accounts. For example, for the ΓÇ£compare fromΓÇÙ version, the user can select from ΓÇ£allocation, actual, aggregate and simulationΓÇÙ, a total of four versions.

 

 

3. Then create a calculated account showing the difference between the two versions. In this case I calculated the variance rate:  (actual-budget)/actual

 

4. Now in the story you have a table with two calculation controls. The users can select which two versions to compare across.

 

 

2. Using LOOKUP Formulas in the Modeler

Previously weΓÇÕve discussed the options to create version comparisons in the story, however if you would like to use this comparisons across different stories, creating a measure in modeler will be the go-to method.

This method applied to the scenario that:

  • You want to compare versions in story
  • This comparison is used across different stories, in multiple charts and tables.
  • The dataset is relatively large and youΓÇÕre pursuing optimized performance.

Follow the steps:

  1. Go to the model youΓÇÕd like to create the comparison from, go to measures and add a new measure, e.g., actual2budget.
  2. In the formula editor, type the following formula to calculate the comparison. In this case, I divided the gross sales of the actual version by the gross sales of the budget version.

3. Now you will have a new measure in your model called actual2budget. It can be used in any story charts and tables.

 

Summary

This guide discussed various options for comparing versions in SAC. What are your commonly used ways of comparing versions? Share your comments below to help complete this guide.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud TDD Scope Item Activation 6W3
entry.content: Thank you

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content: Make sure you have `pillow` package installed in the virtual environment `.venv` and that `.venv` is the kernel you are using to run the notebook.

entry.title: How to extract all Idoc segments for basic type WMMBID01?
entry.content:
In we60, i'm able to extract in HTML format or XML and paste it in Excel. For ARTMAS10, for example, I get all my segments and fiels but when it comes to WMMBID01, I don't get all segments .. Its like it stops in the extraction at header level. So i'm wondering, if there is another way, using tables or somthing else. 

#we60 #idocs #idocmanagement #

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP: How to call protected app across regions with SAML and OAuth [2]: Tutorial
entry.content:
Great blog post, I was able to deploy a CAP project based on this tutorial https://github.com/SAP-samples/fiori-tools-samples/tree/main/cap/cap-fiori-mta and then consume the OData service from a different subaccount on a different region.

Thanks.

John

entry.title: Re: SAP Crypto DLL filling disk space
entry.content:
According to SAP Note 3445494, the problem should be solved in SAP Version 10.0 SP 2311.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3445494

You can try to reinstall the Web client and Service Layer components together. If that doesn't help, then you need to open a ticket about this problem at SAP Support.

entry.title: Adding @UI Annotations to CDS View entity gives OData Error
entry.content:
Hello All,

When I add any @ui annotations to a CDS view / root View entity, then I see red error message at @OData.publish: true . It says,

Multiple markers at this line:
- View has generated objects
- Vocan does not exist [OData Exposure]

 

Could you please someone advise, how can i address this ? I guess it is related to Vocabulary annotation.

Attached the screenshot of the error.

Best Regards !

SAP Fiori Elements

entry.title: Re: Improved ΓÇÌGet SupportΓÇÕ Experience: Enhanced Placement of Detailed Description & AI Recommend
entry.content: Thanks for the info, great news that we strive to further improve efficiency and effectiveness for all involved parties! Hope the test yields positive results!

entry.title: Re: Benefits of using Subcontracting Collaboration in SAP Business Network for Supply Chain
entry.content: With changing supply chain model where we see growth of contract manufacturers to a global brand, a connected network of sub-contracting helps every business entities. From a consultant perspective, we need to mark vendor transactions as 'out of SAP' any more while designing the process. A kudos for @arit_basu for sharing this insight.

entry.title: Controllin on Sap S/4Hana Cloud public edition
entry.content:
Hello Community 
I would like to ask you for information: but on the public edition cloud, are there no specific certifications for the Controlling module? Where can I find study materials?
@Expert @Cloud

entry.title: Re: Enabling your workforce for the future with the new integrated learning experience
entry.content:
Good day,
Is there any functionality from the old learning experience that has not been brought over to the new learning experience?
Thank you

entry.title: Identify performance issue in BW on HANA
entry.content:
Hi All,

Our SAP BW on HANA system have Performance issue where every Transaction is taking much time say like( Going to RSA1, Opening a Process chain and so on) also some times to login in to BW system.

Please let me know a process where i can see all the Process's or Transactions happened on a single day , Memory consumed and Time taken to complete.

So that we can go back to specific user or task and take action to enhance performance

entry.title: Re: Unlock the Potential of AI and Shape the Future: SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2-2024
entry.content: Thanks for the info, great opportunity!

entry.title: Re: advanced payment management: Processing Inbound Messages (PAIN.002) using Multi-Bank Connectivit
entry.content: Good one. Very detailed.

entry.title: Re: Identify performance issue in BW on HANA
entry.content: Please check SAP note 2000000 - FAQ: SAP HANA Performance Optimization - SAP for Me

entry.title: SAP C4C - Add a column of employees active/deacitve indicator
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I want to add a column of employees active/deactive status under people workcenter--Employees wokrcenter veiw main page. Could you please guide me how to do this in SAP C4C? Here is the snap where i want to add a column.

 

Thanks you!!!

 

Regards,

Dhaval Solanki

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content: I have installed in virtual environment '.venv' still system is throwing error saying Module not found.

entry.title: Re: Message "'UI Update' has encountered a problem." appears often while debugging in ADT
entry.content: I'm using DevStyle / Darkest Dark theme with ADT for over two years. But yes, I have noticed some issues, but nothing major. But if recomendation is not to use it, then I guess it's best not to use it. It had really good icons. ≡ÓÌ₧ I'll try with default theme and give a feedback. ≡ÓÀì

entry.title: Re: Signavio SAP Process Manager license clearing
entry.content:
Hello Joergbxyz, 

Thank you for your message.

Unfortunately, there is no automatic solution to remove licences from inactive accounts. However, we can activate a user activity report for you, which will help you to identify inactive users. The removal of these users must then be done manually or via the CSV API. 

If you would like us to activate the report for you, please contact us at signavio_success@sap.com with your Workspace ID.

Best, 

Veronika

entry.title: Created a T-Code for the initial screen in display mode and added radio button for the TMG activity
entry.content:
Hi ,
I need to created a T-Code for the initial screen in display mode and added radio button for the TMG activity?

entry.title: Re: Guidelines for API Journal Entry ΓÇô Post
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to change journal entry via api sending 

 <ItemKey>
               <AccountingDocument>100000007</AccountingDocument>
               <CompanyCode>2210</CompanyCode>
               <FiscalYear>2024</FiscalYear>
               <AccountingDocumentItemID>000001</AccountingDocumentItemID>
</ItemKey>
 
But public cloud gives error 
To be changed journal entry item 2210 100000007 2024 001 could not be found.
 
What I can see I am sending 000001 it change it to 001.
 
Any idea what is issue?
 
thanks

entry.title: Decommision S/4HANA Public Cloud System
entry.content:
We are working to Migrate entities in Public Cloud to another S/4HANA System and decommission existing public cloud system. 

Would be great to know the decommission activities after Migrate(Master Data, Open Orders, Financial Balances). 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Landed cost list not showing while copying from Lande...
entry.content:
Dear @Krish_SAP , 

In general, adjustment or reversal of a Landed Costs doc can only be done by copying the Landed Costs doc into a Landed Costs. 

By definition, you can only copy forward the latest available Landed Costs doc.

Before making adjustment suggest that you try your exact scenario in the demo company database to make sure you get the expected result.

For further consultation, please contact support.

Regards,

Maya

entry.title: Re: Implementing Automated Storage of TM Attachments in the SAP Document Management System
entry.content:
Dear Tom, 

Great insights. I'm wondering, how can the system automatically attach pdf-file to the freight order from a SAP and No-SAP Data Management System?

Many thanks in advance and best regards!

entry.title: Re: QM10/QM11/QM12 No filter can be set after upgrade on Version 2023
entry.content: SNote 3484446 released 20/04/2024

entry.title: Re: Help with formula
entry.content: You can use ADD_DURATION and SUBTRACT_DURATION together with FORMAT_DATETIME_LOCAL to format the date:

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Hello

entry.title: SAP BOFC Report Modification
entry.content:
we're encountering with the SAP BOFC report. Recently, we made modifications to include new nature of accounts. These changes were successfully implemented. However, after assigning the task to a specific region, we noticed that these new accounts are not reflecting in the report when we attempt to run it.

It seems that there might be a configuration or setting that needs adjustment to ensure the new accounts are properly integrated into the report output. Could someone please advise on the necessary steps or settings that may be missing in this process?

Your prompt assistance in resolving this matter would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration - Connect to Microsoft 365 Mail with OAuth2
entry.content: Is there such an blog for connecting SAP Build Process Automation via OAuth2Password tu Microsoft 365?

entry.title: Re: Working with SAP Service Cloud Version 2 Add-on, for Utilities: Master Data Replication Part I
entry.content:
Hi @steen_hansen ,
I am trying to replicate Company Code, Product Group... but i am getting the same error

"Invalid value null for attribute displayId." eventhough the displayId is present in the payload.Please help me with this.

Regards,
Keerthana

entry.title: Re: New Maintenance Service Process in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
Hi @noboruota ,

Is "service maintenance" available in "SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2402" as well?

BR,

Amin

entry.title: Re: Your experiences with APQC PCF
entry.content: Hi John, thanks for this explenation, I have a specific interest to understand what/how Signavio will accomodate further integration with APQC. As you indicated you have created an APQC attribute yourself, I would have expected that Signavio would make an attribute available so integration and future functionalities are thought off: example: APQC unique process id # would link to relevant content in the APQC platform including benchmark/KPIs. Does anyone from Signavio can share plans for further integration? to avoid duplicate effort?

entry.title: Re: AI Use Cases in SAP S/4 Transportation Management
entry.content: Great insights!

entry.title: Re: Wrong delivery date - Quote Order to Sales Order
entry.content:
in sales order it should be 3 days starting from  quote date creation

As rightly pointed out by @jignesh_mehta3 with the standard configuration, this will not happen and seems to be due to custom validation.  You need to take the help of your ABAP team to find out the root cause.

entry.title: Delivery complete indicator reset issue in Service PO
entry.content:
Hi,

In service Purchase order, we are marking line item wise delivery completed indicator manually when we donΓÇÕt require service anymore/ short close of service PO.

When user cancels service entry sheet of that po using tcode ML81N then system will reset delivery complete indicator in service PO. My business wants to restrict it or give warning message at the time of SES reversal.

Request you to suggest way forward

Regards,

Bhaumik Modi

entry.title: Re: Questions on 'Just Ask' in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content: Hi francois_imberton, Thank you so much. This input will help a lot.

entry.title: SAP-VAT CODE-PURCHASE REQUEST
entry.content:
Hi all,

I use SAP- B1

I prepar Purchase Request and I fill ITEM field, SUPPLIER field but VAT CODE field doesn't update in automatic with the SUPPLIER

So we need to check everytime the correct VAT CODE for each item insert in Purchase Request

How can we solve it?

entry.title: Re: Fast entry TAB in Prodution order
entry.content:
Hello @pradeepkumar21 

To my knowledge tabs in production order maintenance transactions e.g. CO02, CO03 are not customizable. Therefore you see the fast entry tab, even if you don't really use it.

If that bothers you, you can hide it using a transaction variant - SAP Help  Transaction Variants and Screen Variants

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: New Maintenance Service Process in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
@Nima_S, 
Hi Amin, no the feature we discussed in this webinar is currently planned to be only available with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise.

entry.title: Re: New Maintenance Service Process in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
Hi Amin,
the Maintenance Service option (in release S/4HANA 2023 we call it now "Service with advanced execution") is not available in any SAP S/4HANA public cloud version, so also not available in SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2402. However this option is available in newer SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud editions. 

Kind regards, Oliver

entry.title: Re: Step-By-Step to run a simple 'Order to Cash' process
entry.content:
Very nice and detailed information, simple and easy to understand.

Thank you..!!
